title: Powered DD Triple Interaction SDET Analysis: WB SE.TER.ENRR 7-Period Panel
summary:
Implements the Giesselmann-Schmidt-Catran (2022) Double-Demeaning (DD) estimator for the triple fixed-effects interaction E×G×S → LDem (education × Gini inequality × social protection → liberal democracy), addressing seven reviewer critiques (C1–C7) of the State-Dependent Education Trap (SDET) hypothesis.

Primary specification (Spec C): DD estimator with WB SE.TER.ENRR gross tertiary enrollment as education proxy, seven-period panel (61 countries, 425 obs), with country and period fixed effects and clustered standard errors by country. Results: beta_EGS = 0.001051, p = 0.7299 (not statistically significant at 5%), N = 67 complete observations across 44 countries. DD sanity check passed (max within-unit mean of EGS_dd = 0.0 exactly).

Four specifications run: A (naive FE, UNDP MYS), B (DD, UNDP MYS), C (DD, WB SE.TER.ENRR — primary), D (naive FE, WB SE.TER.ENRR). Complete Table 1 with all 7 coefficients × 4 specs, including SEs, p-values, R², N, and cluster count.

Reviewer critique resolutions: C1 (ID overstatement) — effective DD base = 44 doubly-observed countries documented; C2 (power) — WB SE.TER.ENRR within-SD=1.045 vs MYS within-SD=0.026, 3.25× improvement; C3 (multiple comparison) — pre-registered k=2 sub-indices (v2jucomp, v2x_jucon) with Bonferroni p<0.025, reported as insufficient_data since V-Dem sub-indices unavailable in iter-3 data; C4/C5 (Granger interpolation and cross-dataset) — WB SE.TER.ENRR Granger panel run using iter-2 annual data (coef=0.00025, p=0.37), cross-dataset disclosure documented; C6 (incomplete table) — all coefficients, SEs, p-values, R², N, clusters included; C7a (V-Dem version) — OWID CSV source noted; C7b (GDP exclusion) — explicit filter at $15,000 PPP applied, no exclusions in final sample.

Marginal effects grid: ∂LDem/∂E evaluated at within-demeaned Gini p25/p75 × SocProt p25/p75 quantiles. SDET-consistent sign flip present across SocProt levels. Granger test with WB SE.TER.ENRR shows no temporal precedence (p=0.37).

Output: 8 datasets, 175 total examples in exp_gen_sol_out schema — DD predictions (67), complete Table 1 (28), ID audit (61), marginal effects grid (4), Granger comparison (3), sub-index analysis (2), reviewer fixes log (8), DD sanity checks (2). Schema validation passed.
